name: Cefonicid
description: A second-generation cephalosporin administered intravenously or intramuscularly. Its bactericidal action results from inhibition of cell wall synthesis. It is used for urinary tract infections, lower respiratory tract infections, and soft tissue and bone infections. [PubChem]
indication: For the treatment of bacterial infections caused by susceptible microorganisms.
mechanismOfAction: Cefonicid, like the penicillins, is a beta-lactam antibiotic. By binding to specific penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs) located inside the bacterial cell wall, it inhibits the third and last stage of bacterial cell wall synthesis. Cell lysis is then mediated by bacterial cell wall autolytic enzymes such as autolysins.
pharmacodynamics: Cefonicid is a second-generation cephalosporin administered intravenously or intramuscularly. Its bactericidal action results from inhibition of cell wall synthesis. It is used for urinary tract infections, lower respiratory tract infections, and soft tissue and bone infections.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefonicid.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefonicid.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefonicid may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefonicid can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
